facade_description: 67 Oxford St presents a house-form, semi-detached facade within the Kensington Market HCD. The street elevation spans roughly 5.2 metres across 3 storeys and is capped by a gable, consistent with its house-form, semi-detached typology. The facade reads primarily as mixed in a cream vertical siding over an altered rear facade with enclosed porch addition palette, laid in running bond with light grey mortar joints. The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 2, 3, 1 window bays from lower to upper levels, using mixed, and 1 principal entrance. Character-defining facade elements include cornice. HCD note: Each property has value as a late-nineteenth- to early-twentieth-century House-form building that reflects vernacular interpretations of Victorian architectural styles. Through its construction date and typology, each property is directly associated with the District’s early residential development
